description: Schema for fact-nodes and term-nodes in the public fact graph. The presentation layer (HTML) holds ZERO facts; it references these nodes by id. Every fact-node must trace to its origin via evidence. Status grades are distinct and must never be conflated.

status_grades.candidate: Proposed but not yet tested or accepted. Lowest grade.
status_grades.proved: Demonstrated by a passing test/proof in a source repo, with linked evidence. Does NOT imply canonical release.
status_grades.released: Packaged as a canonical release artifact (e.g. transition-table v1-rc1). Implies proved.
status_grades.canonical: The authoritative ecosystem record. Implies released.
status_grades.hypothesis: Conjectured, not proved. May be published only when explicitly labelled as hypothesis.

origin_types.artifact: Fact is substantiated by repo artifacts (reports, receipts). Requires evidence paths that resolve. Used for proved/released/canonical facts derived from tests.
origin_types.declaration: Fact is a governing declaration (a boundary statement, an authority rule, an entity-nomenclature fact). Its origin is the declaration itself, not a test artifact. Requires a 'source' naming the governing authority; does not require file evidence.

fields.fact_id: Stable dotted identifier, e.g. 'roadmap.stages_passed'. Never changes; the value behind it may.
fields.value: The published value (string, number, or short structured object).
fields.status: One of status_grades. The grade of claim this fact makes.
fields.origin_type: One of origin_types. 'artifact' facts require resolving evidence; 'declaration' facts do not.
fields.source: The repo, artifact, or governing authority of origin. Where the fact is born.
fields.evidence: Array of artifact paths that substantiate the value. Required when origin_type is 'artifact' and status is proved/released/canonical. A fact whose required evidence does not resolve must not be published.
fields.verified_on: ISO date the fact was last checked against its evidence or re-affirmed as declaration (the match-gate timestamp).
fields.supersedes: Optional fact_id (optionally @date) of the fact this replaces.
fields.label: Optional human-readable short label for presentation.
fields.note: Optional clarifying note, e.g. a stated ambiguity. If a fact's status cannot be stated, it is not published.

fields.term_id: Stable identifier for a vocabulary term, e.g. 'admissibility_space'.
fields.term: The display form of the term.
fields.definition: Canonical definition. Defined once here; every page links to it.
fields.introduced_in: Stage/paper/artifact where the term was first introduced.
fields.supersedes: Optional term_id this term renames or replaces.

fields.relation: relation_id drawn from relations.json. Edges using an unknown relation are invalid.

publication_rules: A page may render a fact only by fact_id; it may not contain the fact's literal value.
A fact with status proved/released/canonical must have at least one resolving evidence path.
Every edge's relation must exist in relations.json (closed set).
A term used in any published fact must have a term_node.
If a fact's status cannot be stated clearly, the fact is not published.
